title: SQL Injection via SqlCommand with string concatenation
summary: User input flows into a SqlCommand constructor or execution method as part of a concatenated or interpolated SQL string, without parameterized queries. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to read, modify, or delete database contents.
why_it_matters: SQL injection is one of the top web vulnerabilities because it gives direct access to the application's data. Successful exploitation can expose user credentials, PII, financial data, and session tokens, or escalate to operating-system command execution via stored procedures like xp_cmdshell. Automated scanners find these flaws in minutes.
how_to_fix:
Use parameterized queries in every database call. SqlCommand supports this via the Parameters collection:

1. Construct the SqlCommand with named parameters (`@id`, `@email`) in the SQL string.
2. For each parameter, call cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue("@name", value) or use a typed SqlParameter with an explicit SqlDbType.
3. Never interpolate or concatenate user input into the SQL string — treat `$"SELECT ... {value}"` the same as string concatenation.

For bulk operations or ORMs, use Dapper (Execute, Query with parameters) or Entity Framework with LINQ — both are parameterized by default.
developer_notes: String interpolation ($"...") looks safer than concatenation ("..." + x) but is exactly equivalent for SQL injection purposes — the compiler turns it into string.Format at compile time. Use FormattableString with SqlClient helpers (e.g., FormattableStringFactory) only when the receiving API is explicitly documented to parameterize the arguments.
verification_checklist: No string concatenation of user input into SQL strings
No $"..." interpolation of user input into SQL strings
Every parameter uses cmd.Parameters.AddWithValue or a typed SqlParameter
Stored procedure calls pass arguments as parameters, not in the SQL string
ORM calls (Dapper, EF) use named parameters, never Raw SQL with interpolated input

references.title: OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
